audit_items.proof_trace: `ldap_nameform2bv` calls `print_oids(ss, nf->nf_at_oids_must)` at L836 without a NULL guard, while all other callers of `print_oids` guard with `if (field) { ... }`. `nf->nf_at_oids_must` can be NULL when `ldap_str2nameform` parses a MUST clause with empty parens (`MUST ()`): `parse_oids` returns NULL via the TK_RIGHTPAREN branch (L1365-1369) without modifying `*code`, so the check `if (!nf_at_oids_must && *code != LDAP_SUCCESS)` does not trigger (code stays 0), the struct is returned with a NULL field, and subsequent `print_oids(ss, NULL)` dereferences NULL at `sa[0]` (L305).
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker controlling an LDAP server (or performing MITM) sends a nameform schema definition containing `MUST ()` (empty MUST OID list); the LDAP client calls `ldap_str2nameform` which succeeds and returns an `nf` struct with `nf_at_oids_must == NULL`, then calls `ldap_nameform2bv` to pretty-print it, triggering a NULL pointer dereference (SIGSEGV) and crashing the client process.
